prompt:
Add DNS management for gunk.dev via Porkbun API — using Go for actuation instead of bash scripts.

## Context
gunk-dev/infra manages Fly.io deployments via CUE + Nix flakes. We're adding DNS record management for gunk.dev hosted on Porkbun. The repo has no Go tooling yet.

## Existing DNS records to preserve
These records already exist on Porkbun and MUST be included in the CUE definition so they're managed as IaC:

### Fastmail DKIM (CNAME)
- fm1._domainkey -> fm1.gunk.dev.dkim.fmhosted.com (TTL 600)
- fm2._domainkey -> fm2.gunk.dev.dkim.fmhosted.com (TTL 600)
- fm3._domainkey -> fm3.gunk.dev.dkim.fmhosted.com (TTL 600)

### Fastmail MX (bare domain, i.e. name is empty string or '@')
- in1-smtp.messagingengine.com, priority 10, TTL 600
- in2-smtp.messagingengine.com, priority 20, TTL 600

### TXT (bare domain)
- google-site-verification=26VQTRvP0Q7wB3mhQPg4FRRKqMLMXq7US_kL2OxE0Nw (TTL 600)
- v=spf1 include:spf.messagingengine.com ?all (TTL 600)

### App CNAME records
- flux -> flux-prod.fly.dev
- staging.flux -> flux-staging.fly.dev
- balance -> balance-prod.fly.dev
- staging.balance -> balance-staging.fly.dev

## What to create

### 1. CUE schema for DNS records
Update schema/dns.cue with a #DNSRecord schema. Include a 'priority' field (optional, for MX records):
- type: 'A' | 'CNAME' | 'TXT' | 'MX' | 'AAAA'
- name: string (subdomain, e.g., 'flux', empty string for bare domain)
- content: string
- ttl: int | *600
- priority: int | *null (only used for MX)

### 2. DNS record definitions
Create dns/gunk.dev.cue with ALL records listed above — both the app CNAMEs and the existing Fastmail/email records.

### 3. Go CLI tool for DNS sync
Create a Go module at cmd/dns/ (or tools/dns/) with subcommands:
- `dns sync` — reads CUE export (via stdin or cue export ./dns --out json piped in), fetches current records from Porkbun API, creates/updates/deletes to converge. Only prune records not in CUE when --prune flag is passed. When pruning, skip NS/SOA records and preview-* subdomain records (managed dynamically).
- `dns preview create <app> <pr-number>` — creates a CNAME: preview-{pr}.{app}.gunk.dev -> {app}-preview-{pr}.fly.dev
- `dns preview delete <app> <pr-number>` — deletes the preview CNAME

Use cobra for CLI structure. Use PORKBUN_API_KEY and PORKBUN_SECRET_KEY env vars for auth.

Porkbun API endpoints (all POST, all require apikey+secretapikey in body):
- Retrieve: https://api.porkbun.com/api/json/v3/dns/retrieve/{domain}
- Create: https://api.porkbun.com/api/json/v3/dns/create/{domain}
- Edit by name/type: https://api.porkbun.com/api/json/v3/dns/editByNameType/{domain}/{type}/{subdomain}
- Delete by name/type: https://api.porkbun.com/api/json/v3/dns/deleteByNameType/{domain}/{type}/{subdomain}
- Delete by ID: https://api.porkbun.com/api/json/v3/dns/delete/{domain}/{id}

### 4. Remove bash scripts
Delete scripts/dns-sync.sh and scripts/dns-preview.sh. They were added in a branch but not merged to main, so they may not exist on main — only delete if present.

### 5. Update workflows
Update .github/workflows/dns.yml (create if not exists) to:
- Build the Go DNS tool
- Run: cue export ./dns --out json | go run ./cmd/dns sync
- Triggered on push to main when dns/ or cmd/dns/ changes

Update preview workflows (.github/workflows/preview.yml and balance-preview.yml) to use the Go tool instead of bash for preview DNS create/delete.

### 6. Update flake.nix
Add go and jq to the dev shell packages.

### 7. go.mod
Initialize a Go module. Use module path gunk.dev/infra.

### 8. Update README
Document DNS management: how records are defined in CUE, how sync works, how to run manually.

## Constraints
- No Terraform. CUE for declaration, Go for actuation.
- The Go code should be clean and straightforward — no over-abstraction.
- Run cue vet and go build before committing.
- Pin action SHAs in any new/modified workflows.
- Add explicit permissions blocks to workflow jobs.
